root.title: Turn 1: The Casual Hook
root.text: Let's figure out what's going on with you today. Pick the word that hits closest to your energy right now.

node_t2_overwhelmed.title: Turn 2: Overwhelmed Validation
node_t2_overwhelmed.text: That's a lot to deal with. Usually, when things feel that heavy, it's because you're trying to manage a bunch of expectations that shouldn't even be yours to fix. Does that sound about right?

node_t2_bored.title: Turn 2: Bored Validation
node_t2_bored.text: That's a weirdly frustrating space to be in. People think boredom is peaceful, but it usually means you're completely drained by routines that don't actually matter to you.

node_t2_fine.title: Turn 2: Fine Validation
node_t2_fine.text: Fair enough. But honestly, sometimes saying you're 'fine' is just an easy defense mechanism to shut everything else out so you don't have to break your focus.

node_t3_funnel.title: Turn 3: The Mind Drift
node_t3_funnel.text: When you finally get a quiet second alone with your thoughts, where does your brain usually end up drifting?

node_t4_future.title: Turn 4: Future Response
node_t4_future.text: It almost sounds like you're trying to solve problems that haven't even happened yet—like you're constantly stressing over a script that hasn't even been written.

node_t4_past.title: Turn 4: Past Response
node_t4_past.text: It's incredibly exhausting to live like that. You keep reviewing old footage, playing back conversations or choices, and wishing you could rewrite a script that's already finished.

node_t5_pressure_split.title: Turn 5: The Pressure Source
node_t5_pressure_split.text: There's usually a specific pressure pulling at your attention when that happens. Is it mostly tied to school and grades, or is it more of a social, friend group kind of thing?

node_t6_school.title: Turn 6: Academic Focus
node_t6_school.text: Yeah, the academic weight. It's like there's this constant background noise telling you that a single grade or test score is going to dictate your entire life direction.

node_t6_social.title: Turn 6: Social Focus
node_t6_social.text: The social side of things is so messy. Trying to fit into a dynamic, worrying about what people think, or dealing with subtle drama takes up way more mental energy than people admit.

node_t7_target_split.title: Turn 7: The Root Fear
node_t7_target_split.text: When you look closely at where that pressure is actually coming from, what feels like the absolute worst part of it?

node_t8_others.title: Turn 8: External Validation
node_t8_others.text: That's a heavy burden. You end up managing everyone else's potential reactions and anxiety instead of just giving yourself the space to figure out what you need.

node_t8_self.title: Turn 8: Internal Validation
node_t8_self.text: That's a very quiet, lonely kind of stress. When you are your own harshest critic, you never get a break. Even when you do well, your brain just moves the goalposts away.

node_t9_internal_funnel.title: Turn 9: Internalized Standards
node_t9_internal_funnel.text: Be totally honest: Do you feel like these massive stakes are actually being forced on you right now, or have you just internalized the idea that anything less than perfect is a total failure?

node_t10_internalized.title: Turn 10: Judge Feedback
node_t10_internalized.text: It sounds like you've become your own toughest boss. You're holding yourself to a standard of performance that you would probably never demand from a close friend.

node_t10_outside.title: Turn 10: System Feedback
node_t10_outside.text: It definitely feels like the environment around you is running like a pressure cooker. It forces you to constantly compete, compare, and measure your daily worth against everyone else.

node_t11_coping_split.title: Turn 11: The Reaction
node_t11_coping_split.text: When that underlying stress starts building up and acting up in your routine, how does it usually warp your day-to-day life?

node_t12_freeze.title: Turn 12: Freeze Response
node_t12_freeze.text: Freezing is a classic defense mechanism. If you don't actually start the task, your mind thinks it's protecting you from failing at it. But it just keeps you trapped in the anxiety.

node_t12_crash.title: Turn 12: Crash Response
node_t12_crash.text: Running on pure adrenaline only works for a little while. You keep pushing because you're terrified that if you slow down for even an hour, the whole structure will fall apart.

node_t13_escape_funnel.title: Turn 13: High-Stress Resting
node_t13_escape_funnel.text: And when you try to escape that cycle by scrolling on your phone or isolating in your room, it never actually feels like a break, does it? It's 'high-stress resting'—your body is still, but your mind is still burning massive amounts of fuel.

node_t14_magic_button.title: Turn 14: The Obligation Check
node_t14_magic_button.text: Imagine if you could magically delete just one major obligation or social expectation from your plate today. If that button were right in front of you, would you honestly press it?

node_t15_end.title: Turn 15: The Final Mirror
node_t15_end.text: The fact that we can trace your habits all the way from how you walked into this room to how you handle your deepest pressures shows how real these patterns are. Whether you're stuck reviewing yesterday or racing into tomorrow, you're missing right now. You don't have to carry it all perfectly. Put down the script, close this tab, and go take one small, imperfect action. You're going to be okay.
